content.problemStatement:
For a given tree TTT with NNN nodes and a permutation PPP of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N], we define %\text{score}(P)$ as follows:


Let SXS_XSX​ denote the set of nodes that are either XXX itself, or adjacent to XXX.
Let AXA_XAX​ denote the median†^{\dagger}† of the set {Py:y∈SX}\{P_y: y \in S_X\}{Py​:y∈SX​}.
score(P)=\text{score}(P) = score(P)= number of integers XXX (1≤X≤N1 \le X \le N1≤X≤N) such that AX=PXA_X = P_XAX​=PX​.
For the given tree, find the maximum possible value of score(P)\text{score}(P)score(P) over all permutations of [1,N][1, N][1,N].

A permutation of [1,N][1, N][1,N] is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN exactly once.

The tree is given in the form of par2,par3,…,parN\text{par}_2, \text{par}_3, \ldots, \text{par}_Npar2​,par3​,…,parN​ such that 1≤pari<i1 \le \text{par}_i < i1≤pari​<i, and there is an edge between (pari,i)(\text{par}_i, i)(pari​,i) for each 2≤i≤N2 \le i \le N2≤i≤N.

†^{\dagger}† The median of a set with KKK distinct elements, is the ⌊K+12⌋\left\lfloor \frac{K + 1}{2} \right\rfloor⌊2K+1​⌋-th smallest element. For example, the median of {1,2}\{1, 2\}{1,2} is 111 and the median of {1,9,8}\{1, 9, 8\}{1,9,8} is 888.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 111 integer NNN - the size of the tree.
The second line contains N−1N - 1N−1 integers, par2,par3,…,parN\text{par}_2, \text{par}_3, \ldots, \text{par}_Npar2​,par3​,…,parN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible score over all permutations.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Both P=[1,2]P = [1, 2]P=[1,2] and P=[2,1]P = [2, 1]P=[2,1] have a score of 111. In the first case, X=1X = 1X=1 satisfies the condition, and in the second X=2X = 2X=2 satisfies it.
Test case 222: P=[3,1,2]P = [3, 1, 2]P=[3,1,2] has a score of 222.
